title: Inside the court of France's richest man, Bernard Arnault
subhead: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (1/6). The billionaire at the head of LVMH is as secretive as he is powerful. He relies on a devoted entourage in which a mix of fear and admiration prevails.

title: Didier Deschamps bids farewell to French team after spectacular World Cup defeat
'Can we continue to entrust global sport to organizations more concerned with their financial interests than with the public good?'
Rubio hails Lebanon for peace efforts after meeting president
subhead: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio commended the Lebanese government for its 'determined effort to reclaim Lebanon's sovereignty, disarm Hezbollah and dismantle its terrorist infrastructure, and move towards peace.'

title: An organic farmer's dismay at the speed of climate change: 'I thought I would have time to prepare'
subhead: Late frosts, cold springs and repeated heatwaves: Climate hazards now accumulate within a single season, disrupting crops and undermining the livelihoods of vegetable growers who already earn little. Even farms practicing organic agriculture, which are committed to adaptation, are struggling to cope.

title: Floating igloo leaves France for polar exploration mission
subhead: The Tara Polar Station set out from France on Sunday to begin a long-term mission to study biodiversity in the Arctic. The floating laboratory, shaped like an igloo, is designed to withstand sea-ice pressure and to endure temperatures as low as -52º.

title: 20 men, a 'sperm party,' and two days of sexual torment: The brutality of the 'libertine' rapes case
subhead: Over the course of a long weekend, one of Christophe B.'s former partners was forced to endure sexual assaults by numerous men at their home. Some of these assaults were broadcast live on the swinging website Wyylde.
byline: Jérôme Lefilliâtre and  Lorraine de Foucher

subhead: The spread of Ukrainian attacks across the country is deepening Russians' concerns about the regime's ability to protect their basic needs, as the economic situation deteriorates.

title: 19 men charged in mass rapes case among 'libertines' in France
subhead: A man identified as Christophe B. is accused of having forced six former partners into extremely violent sexual encounters with dozens of men recruited online over a period of more than 20 years, under the guise of 'libertinism.'
byline: Jérôme Lefilliâtre and  Lorraine de Foucher

title: Bayeux Tapestry arrives in London for forthcoming display at British Museum
subhead: The thousand-year-old, 70-meter-long embroidery depicting William the Conqueror's exploits in medieval England has been removed from its crate after its transfer from France. Staff will begin a thorough check of its condition on Monday, and it will be placed in a custom-made display case a few days later.
